free_text: Measurements and parametrization of the 16O(d,d0)16O
differential cross section
5.5 MV Tandem Accelerator Laboratory
For the determination of the energy to which the
cross-section values were attributed the energy loss
and the straggling inside the target were calculated.
The target thickness ratio of Au to O was determined
by the p-EBS technique.
Five Silicon Surface Barrier (SSB) detectors,
having a thickness of 500 um, which were mounted at
A self-supporting thin carbon foil
with Na2HPO4 powder evaporated on top, along with a
thin Au layer for normalization purposes.
A scaling factor was applied at each scattering angle.
The scaling factor was calculated as average value
of the ratio between SigmaCalc evaluation for the
natO(d,d0) cross section and the Rutherford scattering
cross section on Au at each beam energy in the energy
range 1500-1980 keV.
Values of the scaling factor for each scattering angle
Angle    Scaling factor     Relative uncertainty
(degree)                               (%)
The combined statistical uncertainties
The systematic uncertainties originating from
the differences between compiled and experimental
stopping power data and their corresponding values
were estimated to be for oxygen - 2.8%
The systematic uncertainties originating from
the differences between compiled and experimental
stopping power data and their corresponding values
were estimated to be for gold - 3.6%
The uncertainty of the scaling factor.
